response.justification: While Bogdan has strong credentials as a senior engineer with 20 years of experience and multiple Staff-level roles, his background is primarily in full-stack web and backend engineering with breadth across multiple domains. The Principal Engineer role specifically requires deep AI expertise with demonstrated products and platforms built with AI—areas where his experience is minimal. His current re:cinq role (just started April 2025) mentions AI-agent infrastructure work, but this is too recent to assess depth, and there's no evidence of prior AI/ML products, no Master's/PhD in an AI-related field, and no demonstrated ability to advise strategic customers on AI adoption. He's a strong general engineer but not positioned for the AI Strategy specialization this role demands.
strengths: 20 years of software engineering experience across multiple domains (web, backend, infrastructure, embedded) exceeding the 15-year requirement
Proven leadership: multiple Staff-level positions, founder of GISCollective (10-year active project), track record of mentoring through workshops and code kata sessions
Strong infrastructure and cloud foundation: AWS CDK, Kubernetes, Docker, CI/CD, DevOps—useful context for understanding cloud platforms
concerns: Minimal AI/ML expertise: current AI-agent infrastructure work is only 1-2 months old with no prior ML/AI product background, and no formal AI education despite this being the core focus area
No demonstrated AI products or platforms: the role requires 'recognized expertise in AI with demonstrated products or platforms,' which is entirely absent from his resume

Career breadth over depth: background spans web, mobile, desktop, embedded, and now AI—suggesting a generalist engineer rather than a specialized strategist equipped to advise on cutting-edge AI adoption
